question.content: In a Drosophila gene A insertion design, a P-element cassette containing GAL4, GFP, and FRT sites is inserted into exon 2. After crossing with a FLP-expressing line, which recombination site mediates the excision of the GFP segment in selected cells?. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Use ASCII characters only. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Reconstruct the insertion architecture in exon 2 and mark the module order around GFP.
step_1.key_point: The GFP block is positioned between two identical recombination symbols.

step_3.content: Apply FLP to the paired recombination symbols to determine which site pair is recognized.
step_3.key_point: FLP acts on FRT-FRT pairs and drives site-specific recombination.

step_5.content: Track the product after recombination: the GFP-containing fragment is excised, and one site remains at the locus.
step_5.key_point: The removed loop corresponds to the region between the two FRT sites.

step_7.content: Identify the recombination-site name associated with FLP in this construct.
step_7.key_point: The required single-expression output is the site symbol itself.
